contentBlocks.original:
ग्रहाणामादिरादित्यो लोकरक्षणकारकः ।
विषमस्थानसम्भूतां पीडां हरतु मे रविः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
grahāṇāmādirādityō lōkarakṣaṇakārakaḥ ।
viṣamasthānasambhūtāṃ pīḍāṃ haratu mē raviḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Āditya (the Sun) is the first among the planets, the protector of the world. May Ravi, afflicting from an inauspicious station, remove my suffering. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
रोहिणीशः सुधामूर्तिः सुधागात्रः सुधाशनः ।
विषमस्थानसम्भूतां पीडां हरतु मे विधुः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rōhiṇīśaḥ sudhāmūrtiḥ sudhāgātraḥ sudhāśanaḥ ।
viṣamasthānasambhūtāṃ pīḍāṃ haratu mē vidhuḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The lord of Rohiṇī, of nectar-form, with a nectar-body, nourished by nectar — may Vidhu (the Moon), afflicting from an inauspicious station, remove my suffering. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
भूमिपुत्रो महातेजा जगतां भयकृत् सदा ।
वृष्टिकृद्वृष्टिहर्ता च पीडां हरतु मे कुजः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūmiputrō mahātējā jagatāṃ bhayakṛt sadā ।
vṛṣṭikṛdvṛṣṭihartā cha pīḍāṃ haratu mē kujaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The son of the earth, of great radiance, ever causing fear to the world, maker and withholder of rain — may Kuja (Mars) remove my suffering. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
उत्पातरूपो जगतां चन्द्रपुत्रो महाद्युतिः ।
सूर्यप्रियकरो विद्वान् पीडां हरतु मे बुधः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
utpātarūpō jagatāṃ chandraputrō mahādyutiḥ ।
sūryapriyakarō vidvān pīḍāṃ haratu mē budhaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of portentous form for the world, son of the Moon, of great brilliance, the one dear to the Sun — may wise Budha (Mercury) remove my suffering. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
देवमन्त्री विशालाक्षः सदा लोकहिते रतः ।
अनेकशिष्यसम्पूर्णः पीडां हरतु मे गुरुः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvamantrī viśālākṣaḥ sadā lōkahitē rataḥ ।
anēkaśiṣyasampūrṇaḥ pīḍāṃ haratu mē guruḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The counsellor of the gods, wide-eyed, ever devoted to the welfare of the world, attended by countless disciples — may Guru (Jupiter) remove my suffering. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
दैत्यमन्त्री गुरुस्तेषां प्राणदश्च महामतिः ।
प्रभुस्ताराग्रहाणां च पीडां हरतु मे भृगुः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daityamantrī gurustēṣāṃ prāṇadaścha mahāmatiḥ ।
prabhustārāgrahāṇāṃ cha pīḍāṃ haratu mē bhṛguḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The counsellor of the demons, their great preceptor, giver of life, of great wisdom, master of the Tārā (star) planets — may Bhṛgu (Venus) remove my suffering. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
सूर्यपुत्रो दीर्घदेहो विशालाक्षः शिवप्रियः ।
मन्दचारः प्रसन्नात्मा पीडां हरतु मे शनिः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūryaputrō dīrghadēhō viśālākṣaḥ śivapriyaḥ ।
mandachāraḥ prasannātmā pīḍāṃ haratu mē śaniḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Son of the Sun, of long body, wide-eyed, beloved of Śiva, of slow movement, of serene spirit — may Śani (Saturn) remove my suffering. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
महाशिरा महावक्त्रो दीर्घदंष्ट्रो महाबलः ।
अतनुश्चोर्ध्वकेशश्च पीडां हरतु मे शिखी ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāśirā mahāvaktrō dīrghadaṃṣṭrō mahābalaḥ ।
atanuśchōrdhvakēśaścha pīḍāṃ haratu mē śikhī ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of great head, great mouth, long fangs, of great strength, bodiless, with hair pointing upward — may Śikhī (Rāhu) remove my suffering. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
अनेकरूपवर्णैश्च शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।
उत्पातरूपो जगतां पीडां हरतु मे तमः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anēkarūpavarṇaiścha śataśō'tha sahasraśaḥ ।
utpātarūpō jagatāṃ pīḍāṃ haratu mē tamaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of many forms and colours, by hundreds and thousands, of portentous form for all the worlds — may Tamas (Ketu) remove my suffering. (9)
